{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八十神 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 八十神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = [[天之冬衣神]] }} '''八十神'''（やそがみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神々]]の総称。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』、『[[出雲国風土記]]』に記述されている。
『古事記』において[[大穴牟遅神]]の兄弟とされ<ref>広辞苑</ref>、神話では大穴牟遅神を荷物持ちとして[[八上比売]]への求婚する旅に出かけたとされる。その途中、海辺で怪我をして泣いている[[因幡の白兎|赤裸の兎]]を見つけるが、海水に浸かり風に当たれば治るという嘘の治療法を教え、兎を更に苦しませる。その後八十神達は次々に八上比売へと求婚するが悉く断られ、逆に八上比売は八十神達の後で兎を助けた大穴牟神と結婚することを宣言する。逆恨みした八十神達は、山の大きな猪を追い落とすから下で受け止めるようにと大穴牟遅神を脅し、真っ赤に焼けた大岩を落として大穴牟遅神を殺してしまう。母親の[[刺国若比売]]の働きかけによって一段と麗しくなって蘇生した大穴牟遅神だが、再び八十神達の仕掛けた罠によって命を落とす。再び蘇生させた刺国若比売は、木の国の[[大屋毘古神]]の元へ逃げるよう勧めるが、そこへも八十神達は追いかけていく。そこで[[根の国|根之堅洲国]]へ逃れ、遠つ祖の[[須佐之男命]]の試練を受け、その娘の[[須勢理毘売命]]と結婚した大穴牟遅神は、再び地上に戻ると須佐之男命の神宝である[[生大刀]]、[[生弓矢]]を使って八十神達を平定し、地上の主・大国主神となった。
『出雲国風土記』の[[大原郡]]来次郷では、所造天下大神（大国主神）が「八十神は、青垣山の裏にはいさせまい」と言って追い払ったとされる。
神名の「八十」とは「多くの」という意味であり、八十神とは「多くの神々」という意味で具体的な数ではない。
== 系譜 == [[大穴牟遅神]]の兄弟とあることから父は[[天之冬衣神]]とされるが、その母達は不明。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[出雲族]] * [[大国主の神話]] * [[因幡の白兎]]
{{デフォルトソート:やそがみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]]

{{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八十神 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 八十神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = [[天之冬衣神]] }} '''八十神'''（やそがみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神々]]の総称。

『出雲国風土記』の[[大原郡]]来次郷では、所造天下大神（大国主神）が「八十神は、青垣山の裏にはいさせまい」と言って追い払ったとされる。
神名の「八十」とは「多くの」という意味であり、八十神とは「多くの神々」という意味で具体的な数ではない。
== 系譜 == [[大穴牟遅神]]の兄弟とあることから父は[[天之冬衣神]]とされるが、その母達は不明。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[出雲族]] * [[大国主の神話]] * [[因幡の白兎]]

{{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Amanofuyukoromikami|ja|天之冬衣神}} }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of {{ill|Ōnamuji|ja|大穴牟遅神}} (later known as Ōkuninushi). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuji to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuji, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuji, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuji’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuji was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuji escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuji and thus sons of {{ill|Amanofuyukoromikami|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi’s mythology|ja|大国主の神話}} * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}}

Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuji, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuji, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuji’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuji was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuji and thus sons of {{ill|Amanofuyukoromikami|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.

== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. {{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}} }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.

{{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}} }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi’s mythology|ja|大国主の神話}} * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}}

{{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}} }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|出雲国風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.
== Notes == {{Reflist}}

Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.

revision.comment: 八十神（やそがみ）は、日本神話に登場する神々の総称。→八十神（やそがみ）は、日本神話に登場する大穴牟遅神の兄にあたる神々の総称。
revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019年6月16日 (日) 12:17 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八十神 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 八十神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = [[天之冬衣神]] }} '''八十神'''（やそがみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[大穴牟遅神]]の兄にあたる[[神 (神道)|神々]]の総称。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』、『[[出雲国風土記]]』に記述されている。
『古事記』において[[大穴牟遅神]]の兄弟とされ<ref>広辞苑</ref>、神話では大穴牟遅神を荷物持ちとして[[八上比売]]への求婚する旅に出かけたとされる。その途中、海辺で怪我をして泣いている[[因幡の白兎|赤裸の兎]]を見つけるが、海水に浸かり風に当たれば治るという嘘の治療法を教え、兎を更に苦しませる。その後八十神達は次々に八上比売へと求婚するが悉く断られ、逆に八上比売は八十神達の後で兎を助けた大穴牟神と結婚することを宣言する。逆恨みした八十神達は、山の大きな猪を追い落とすから下で受け止めるようにと大穴牟遅神を脅し、真っ赤に焼けた大岩を落として大穴牟遅神を殺してしまう。母親の[[刺国若比売]]の働きかけによって一段と麗しくなって蘇生した大穴牟遅神だが、再び八十神達の仕掛けた罠によって命を落とす。再び蘇生させた刺国若比売は、木の国の[[大屋毘古神]]の元へ逃げるよう勧めるが、そこへも八十神達は追いかけていく。そこで[[根の国|根之堅洲国]]へ逃れ、遠つ祖の[[須佐之男命]]の試練を受け、その娘の[[須勢理毘売命]]と結婚した大穴牟遅神は、再び地上に戻ると須佐之男命の神宝である[[生大刀]]、[[生弓矢]]を使って八十神達を平定し、地上の主・大国主神となった。
『出雲国風土記』の[[大原郡]]来次郷では、所造天下大神（大国主神）が「八十神は、青垣山の裏にはいさせまい」と言って追い払ったとされる。
神名の「八十」とは「多くの」という意味であり、八十神とは「多くの神々」という意味で具体的な数ではない。
== 系譜 == [[大穴牟遅神]]の兄弟とあることから父は[[天之冬衣神]]とされるが、その母達は不明。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[出雲族]] * [[大国主の神話]] * [[因幡の白兎]]
{{日本神話}} {{デフォルトソート:やそがみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Ōyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神}}, though their mothers are not specified.

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, though their mothers are not specified.
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi’s mythology|ja|大国主の神話}} * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎}}

In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=draft}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=draft}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=draft}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=draft}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=draft}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|12=draft}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=draft}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=draft}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=draft}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=draft}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}}, though their mothers are not specified.

The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|12=draft}}, though their mothers are not specified.

revision.text:
{{出典の明記|date=2019年6月16日 (日) 12:17 (UTC)}} {{基礎情報 日本の神 | 名 = 八十神 | 神祇 = [[国津神]] | 全名 = 八十神 | 別名 = | 別称 = | 神階 = | 神格 = | 陵所 = | 父 = [[天之冬衣神]] }} '''八十神'''（やそがみ）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[大穴牟遅神]]の兄にあたる[[神 (神道)|神々]]の総称。
== 概要 == 『[[古事記]]』、『[[出雲国風土記]]』に記述されている。
『古事記』において大穴牟遅神の兄弟とされ<ref>広辞苑</ref>、神話では大穴牟遅神を荷物持ちとして[[八上比売]]への求婚する旅に出かけたとされる。その途中、海辺で怪我をして泣いている[[因幡の白兎|赤裸の兎]]を見つけるが、海水に浸かり風に当たれば治るという嘘の治療法を教え、兎を更に苦しませる。その後八十神達は次々に八上比売へと求婚するが悉く断られ、逆に八上比売は八十神達の後で兎を助けた大穴牟神と結婚することを宣言する。逆恨みした八十神達は、山の大きな猪を追い落とすから下で受け止めるようにと大穴牟遅神を脅し、真っ赤に焼けた大岩を落として大穴牟遅神を殺してしまう。母親の[[刺国若比売]]の働きかけによって一段と麗しくなって蘇生した大穴牟遅神だが、再び八十神達の仕掛けた罠によって命を落とす。再び蘇生させた刺国若比売は、木の国の[[大屋毘古神]]の元へ逃げるよう勧めるが、そこへも八十神達は追いかけていく。そこで[[根の国|根之堅洲国]]へ逃れ、遠つ祖の[[須佐之男命]]の試練を受け、その娘の[[須勢理毘売命]]と結婚した大穴牟遅神は、再び地上に戻ると須佐之男命の神宝である[[生大刀]]、[[生弓矢]]を使って八十神達を平定し、地上の主・大国主神となった。
『出雲国風土記』の[[大原郡]]来次郷では、所造天下大神（大国主神）が「八十神は、青垣山の裏にはいさせまい」と言って追い払ったとされる。
神名の「八十」とは「多くの」という意味であり、八十神とは「多くの神々」という意味で具体的な数ではない。
== 系譜 == [[大穴牟遅神]]の兄弟とあることから父は[[天之冬衣神]]とされるが、その母達は不明。
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 関連項目 == * [[出雲族]] * [[大国主の神話]] * [[因幡の白兎]]
{{日本神話}} {{デフォルトソート:やそがみ}} [[Category:国津神]] [[Category:日本の神]] [[Category:十七世神]]

『出雲国風土記』の[[大原郡]]来次郷では、所造天下大神（大国主神）が「八十神は、青垣山の裏にはいさせまい」と言って追い払ったとされる。

{{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | Rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their mothers are not specified.
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi’s mythology|ja|大国主の神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

{{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | Rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|ja|天之冬衣神|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, though their mothers are not specified.
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi’s mythology|ja|大国主の神話|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Hare of Inaba|ja|因幡の白兎|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{Japanese mythology}}
{{translated page|ja|八十神|version=104190489|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|qq=}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|qq=}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|qq=}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|qq=}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|qq=}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|qq=}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|qq=}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|qq=}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|qq=}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|qq=}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65249011]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | Rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q65249011]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | Rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] | gender= {{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}} | parents= {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|qid=Q48744862}} }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}} * {{ill|Myth of Ōkuninushi|lt=Myth of Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi’s mythology|ja|大国主の神話|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi’s mythology|qq=}} * [[:en:Hare of Inaba|Hare of Inaba]]

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==

The Yasogami are described in both the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition. 

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.

== Genealogy ==

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==

The Yasogami are described in both the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition. 

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.

== Genealogy ==

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition. 

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.

== Genealogy ==

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].

== Overview ==

The Yasogami are described in both the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition. 

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.

== Genealogy ==

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十神]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:八十神]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q65249011]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:29, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Citation needed|date=June 16, 2019}} {{infobox deity | name = Yasogami | type = {{ill|Kunitsukami|ja|国津神|1=Kunitsukami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitsukami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天津神・国津神|comment=confirming this goes to Kunitsukami|1=Kunitsukami|2=zh|3=國神|4=fr|5=Kunitsukami|6=mni|7=ꯀꯨꯅꯤꯠꯁꯨꯀꯃꯤ}} | full name = 八十神 | alternate = | alias = | Rank = | role = | mausoleum = | father = [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]] | gender= {{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}} | parents= {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|WD=Q48744862}} }}
'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in [[:en:Japanese mythology|Japanese mythology]].
== Overview ==
The Yasogami are described in both the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition.
Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.
Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.
In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.
== Genealogy ==
The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of [[:en:Ame-no-Fuyukinu|Ame-no-Fuyukinu]], though their mothers are not specified.

| name        = Yasogami

| parents= {{ill|Ame no Fuyu Kinu no kami|WD=Q48744862}}
}}

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==

The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition. 

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.

== Genealogy ==

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, though their mothers are not specified.

== Notes ==
{{Reflist}}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Izumo clan|ja|出雲族|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo clan|qq=}}
* {{ill|Myth of Ōkuninushi|lt=Myth of Ōkuninushi|lt=Ōkuninushi’s mythology|ja|大国主の神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi’s mythology|qq=}}
* {{ill|Hare of Inaba|en|Hare of Inaba|WD=Q2330988|lt=Hare of Inaba}}

{{Japanese mythology}}

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==

The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki}} and the {{ill|Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo no Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition. 

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.

== Genealogy ==

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, though their mothers are not specified.

'''Yasogami''' (八十神) refers to a collective group of deities mentioned in {{ill|Japanese mythology|en|Japanese mythology|WD=Q814158|lt=Japanese mythology|1=Japanese mythology}}.

== Overview ==

The Yasogami are described in both the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}} and the {{ill|Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo-no-Kuni Fudoki|lt=Izumo Fudoki|ja|Izumo Province 風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Fudoki|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

In the ''Kojiki,'' they are identified as the brothers of Ōnamuchi (later known as [[Ōkuninushi]]). The Yasogami forced Ōnamuchi to act as a porter during their journey to propose marriage to {{ill|Yagami Hime|ja|八上比売|1=Yagami-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami Hime|13=User:Immanuelle/Yagami-hime|qq=}}. Along the way, they encountered a hare suffering from wounds by the seaside. Instead of helping, they gave false advice, worsening the hare’s condition. 

Subsequently, the Yasogami each attempted to woo Yagami Hime, only to be rejected. Instead, Yagami Hime declared her intent to marry Ōnamuchi, who had healed the hare. Enraged, the Yasogami plotted against Ōnamuchi, tricking him into standing below a hill while they rolled down a burning boulder, killing him. Ōnamuchi’s mother, {{ill|Sashikuni Wakahime|ja|刺国若比売|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sashikuni Wakahime|qq=}}, intervened, bringing him back to life even more beautiful than before. However, the Yasogami continued to conspire, leading to his death once more. Revived again by his mother, Ōnamuchi was advised to flee to the land of {{ill|Kigi no Kuni|ja|木の国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kigi no Kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the domain of {{ill|Oyabiko|ja|大屋毘古神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyabiko|qq=}}, but the Yasogami pursued him there as well.

Eventually, Ōnamuchi escaped to the underworld {{ill|Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja|根之堅洲国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ne-no-Katasu-kuni|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where he underwent trials set by {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|須佐之男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}. After succeeding, he married Susanoo’s daughter, {{ill|Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseri-Hime|lt=Suseribime|ja|須勢理毘売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suseribime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スセリビメ}}, and returned to the earthly realm. Using Susanoo’s divine treasures, including the {{ill|Ikutachi|lt=Ikutachi|lt=Life-Giving Sword|ja|生大刀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Sword|qq=}} and {{ill|Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja|生弓矢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Life-Giving Bow and Arrows|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, he defeated the Yasogami, solidifying his position as the earthly ruler, Ōkuninushi.

In the ''Izumo Fudoki,'' the Yasogami are driven out by Ōkuninushi, who declares that they shall not dwell within the shadow of {{ill|Aogaki Mountain|ja|青垣山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aogaki Mountain|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

The term "Yaso" (八十) translates to "many" or "numerous" in this context and does not represent a literal count of deities.

== Genealogy ==

The Yasogami are considered brothers of Ōnamuchi and thus sons of {{ill|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|en|Ame-no-Fuyukinu|WD=Q48744862|lt=Ame-no-Fuyukinu|1=Ame-no-Fuyukinu}}, though their mothers are not specified.
